current_conditions.site_name: SOQUE RIVER AT GA 197, NEAR CLARKESVILLE, GA

generated_text.quick_read: Current river conditions on the Soque River show fair trout fishing with stable flow and moderate gage height. Evening offers the best fishing opportunity due to favorable light and pressure conditions for trout activity.
generated_text.best_window: The day1 evening window is best because light and pressure conditions are balanced, encouraging trout to feed comfortably near runs, seams, and structure with a balanced nymph approach.
generated_text.current_conditions: USGS data reports flow at 64 cfs and gage height at 3.55 ft. Water temperature and turbidity data are unavailable at this time.
generated_text.weather_setup: Sky cover is low to moderate, averaging 16% overall but increasing to 44% in the evening. Atmospheric pressure is steady at 30.03 inHg with a gentle west wind averaging 5.5 mph. Rain probability is minimal with little to no expected rainfall.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout are likely to hold near runs, seams, and structural features, favoring comfortable feeding lanes and proximity to cover during higher light. Use a balanced nymph approach with clean drifts and smaller natural fly patterns. Without recent stocking, rely on natural presentations and stocked-trout attractors selectively. Remain adaptable due to unavailable temperature data.
